NVIDIA
Internship
AV Safety Intern
Summer 2026
Built a DDB visualization plugin that replaces manual safety-critical-event triage math with real-time evasive trajectory rendering — hard steer arcs, an HDA swerve model, and braking corridors on every frame. Formalized the triage question as counterfactual avoidability analysis, grounding reaction-time choices in published disengagement studies.

Selected Projects
Safety systems, control algorithms, robotics, and mechatronics hardware.
Safety
ISO 4804–Aligned MRM Enhancement
Replaced binary fail-safe FSM triggers with a capability-based degradation architecture. Each software component outputs a Quality Level, aggregated by a Capability Checker; a Mode Manager then adjusts the operating mode. Degradation that affects capability triggers an MRM; otherwise it's an HMI warning only — avoiding combinatorial state explosion as the ODD grows.
Functional SafetyFSMMATLAB System Composer
Safety
Auto Parking Manager
Defined feature-level system logic: an end-to-end state machine (IDLE → SEARCHING → APPROACHING → PARKING → DONE), UML sequence diagrams, interface enumerations, and Planning↔Control gating with fail-safe conditions. Delivered a successful live demonstration with partner logistics companies.
FSM DesignUMLInterface Spec
Safety
Regulatory License — EV, Bus & Truck
Led the approval and validation process for 3 autonomous-driving regulatory licenses at the K-City government testbed. Designed test scenarios aligned with government requirements (urban, highway, obstacle, emergency), verified lateral/longitudinal control stability from CAN and sensor logs, and maintained safety documentation with pass/fail criteria traced to each scenario.
Scenario DesignV&VK-City
Control
Disturbance Observer (Q/T-DOB)
Eliminated low-speed deceleration jerk caused by actuator mismatch and road-slope variation. A Q/T-filtered DOB estimates and compensates for unknown disturbances in a first-order vehicle-dynamics model, resolving longitudinal jerking at standstill to enhance ride comfort without retuning the full controller stack.
DOBQ/T FilterRide Comfort
Control
Online Actuator Parameter Estimator
Regression-based system identification deriving numerator/denominator coefficients of a first-order transfer function (~95% accuracy on harness data). Deployed on test vehicles and monitored over 3 months of autonomous driving — detected 3 anomalies, one correctly identified as actuator degradation, leading to a low-level longitudinal controller replacement.
Least SquaresReal-TimeDegradation Detection
Robotics
Real-Time Ball Catching (RGBD + MPC)
A full perception-to-control pipeline on a Kinova Gen3 arm: RealSense RGBD detection → 3D trajectory prediction → MPC arm control. Achieved 33 of 60 catches (55%) in real-hardware trials, demonstrating reliable end-to-end performance under real sensing constraints.
MPCRGBDKinova Gen3ROS
Catch Me If You Can — project site
Mechatronics
Automated Wrap Dispenser
A full mechatronics build: wrapping arm, cutter mechanism, load cell, and limit switches. Designed the arm and pulley transmission in SolidWorks, controlled by embedded firmware sequencing sensor inputs and actuators.
SolidWorksEmbedded CMechanism Design
Mechatronics
CalSol Solar Car — Panel Structure
As Project Leader, designed finger joints in SolidWorks and AutoCAD to connect 80 solar-car panels, running 5 simulations to optimize strength-to-weight against set standards. Integrated the structure across battery, mechanical, and electrical subgroups into a complete vehicle.
SolidWorksAutoCADFEA
Research
UC Berkeley — humanoid robotics, VLA models, and vision-language grounding.
Robotics
Vision–Language Encoder Evaluation
ARC Lab · Prof. Roberto Horowitz
Implemented three text-conditioned heatmap pipelines on RealSense RGB — CLIP ViT-B/16, OpenCLIP RN50, and SigLIP 2 ViT-384 — to expose patch-level spatial language–vision correspondence for peg-in-hole manipulation (EquiContact). Selected OpenCLIP RN50 for the most spatially coherent, viewpoint-stable grounding.
CLIPOpenCLIPSigLIPSpatial Grounding
Robotics
Isaac Lab Humanoid Teleoperation
MSC Lab · Prof. Masayoshi Tomizuka
Imported a CX002 humanoid through a URDF → USD pipeline with a position-control baseline. Built keyboard teleop (incremental joint stepping) and a Tkinter UI (real-time sliders, thread-safe sim–UI sync) for stable 200 Hz whole-body teleoperation — a modular base reusable for IK control, policy learning, and sim-to-real.
Isaac LabURDF → USDPosition Control200 Hz

Publications & Patents
Formal methods for autonomous-driving safety.
Publications
Principal-Filter Framework for Minimal Risk Maneuvers in Autonomous Driving Systems
Yang et al. · RideFlux / UC Berkeley
A formal framework for scalable MRM design. Each MRM is defined by a Minimal Requirement Vector pk; its feasible region is a principal filter, giving monotonicity, consistency, and degrade-only structural guarantees that rule-based FSMs cannot.
Preprint · in review
Patents
Method for Calibrating Truck Sensor Data in Autonomous Driving Environment
Korean Patent Application 10-2025-0121162
A calibration method for aligning multi-sensor data on autonomous trucks, improving perception accuracy under real-world operating conditions.
